NB: is Jason the brother of json 😁
As per the original open api spec and stakeholders request we will only be supporting application/json-patch+json and application/3gpp-json-patch+json
FDN will be the UriLdnFirstPart + ClassName + Id
Should be moved to util class
remove 'withJsonData' java doc description will suffice
'provmns endpoint' same endpoint used for all operations
no need for this after assigning the full FDN to a separate variable in ProvMnsRequestParameters
in case of an error should this be returning 200?
Which error code exactly this api return in case of error? it should not be 200 I believe.
NB: needs to be in line with line 196
NB: you can place a comment like above on line 104, and tell which http operations does it intercept.
it was a code gen issue. its not declared in the docs

Since CmHandleState is ENUM (constant) you can compare them using "==" because it will be faster. It also safer at compile time.

CmHandleState.READY == yangModelCmHandle.getCompositeState().getCmHandleState()

I cannot remember the requirement, but I see you always return 200 (even though you don't make a call towards dmi e.g. for non-ready cm handles).

Was it the requirement that always returning 200?

When I read the API spec for DELETE, you only mentions this:

     * @return Success case "200 OK". This status code is returned, when the resource has been successfully deleted.
     * The response body is empty. (status code 200)

You can have another test for non-ready cm handles. E.g. you can show us how does prov mns interface behaves for ADVISED cm handles? should you still make a call towards dmi for ADVISED cm handles to delete or not?
agrreed, not sure if it is faster bu easier to read anyway
good point about handling non-ready cm handles, lets dicsuss
This is fundamentally caused by the original 3GPP and effectively generated. we cannot change

not ready should be handle as an error , using Sean common new ErrorResponseBuilder for these case
Ignore for now, leave a TO to handle NOT ready later

as agreed this is compliant with yaml definition of the 3GPP interface
BUT we should return the response in status code from the DMI
just return the response from DMI here too
as Toine pointed out no error handling now
can you add TODO's instead on comments or remove?
would have preferred a TODO bu this is OK

howw wdoe this work now, sincce you have a mocked rest client?!

then: 'response is the same as the response from the DMIService'
            assert response.status == responseFromDmi
